intro: Judah upon Jordan is a phrase found in the boundary description of the tribe of Naphtali in Joshua 19:34, where the territory touches "Judah at the Jordan." The reference likely designates a specific ford or district on the eastern bank of the Jordan that marked the boundary point between the tribal territories in the region of the Jordan River's upper course near the Sea of Galilee. The precise identification of the location has been debated by biblical geographers, and it may reflect a now-lost tribal settlement or landmark.
